PXE caused by ABCC6 transporter mutations leading to ectopic mineralization. Affects skin, eyes (angioid streaks), and cardiovascular system with progressive calcification.
Mild PXE variant with incomplete penetrance. May present with isolated angioid streaks or minimal skin findings without full systemic involvement.
Dominant cutis laxa from elastin gene mutations. Features loose, pendulous skin with emphysema and aortic root dilation but generally mild prognosis.
Recessive cutis laxa type 2A with wrinkly skin syndrome phenotype. Features lax skin, developmental delay, and cobblestone-like brain dysplasia.
Cutis laxa type 2B from PYCR1 mutations affecting proline biosynthesis. Progeroid appearance with intellectual disability and intrauterine growth restriction.
Severe cutis laxa type 1C from LTBP4 mutations. Features severe pulmonary emphysema, gastrointestinal and genitourinary anomalies, and often early death.
FOP caused by activating ACVR1/ALK2 mutations triggering heterotopic ossification. Soft tissues progressively transform to bone, severely restricting movement.

condition: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome, Classical Type, COL5A1 Haploinsufficiency
description: Classical EDS from COL5A1 null allele causing haploinsufficiency. Skin hyperextensibility and joint hypermobility with variable wound healing impairment.

condition: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome, Vascular Type, Late Onset
description: Late-onset vascular EDS with milder COL3A1 mutations. Reduced but significant risk of arterial and organ rupture presenting in adulthood.
Isolated ectopia lentis from FBN1 mutations without systemic Marfan features. Bilateral lens subluxation is the primary finding, often requiring surgical correction.
Mitral valve prolapse, borderline aortic enlargement, skin and skeletal findings resembling Marfan syndrome. FBN1 mutation with incomplete marfanoid phenotype.
Familial thoracic aortic aneurysm from TGFBR2 mutations without full LDS features. Significant risk of aortic dissection requiring surveillance.
Isolated familial thoracic aortic aneurysm from TGFBR1 mutations. Features aortic root dilation and risk of dissection without full LDS phenotype.
Aortic aneurysm with early-onset osteoarthritis from SMAD3 mutations. Combines vascular risk with degenerative joint disease.
OI from COL1A1 glycine substitution disrupting collagen triple helix. Severity ranges from moderate to lethal depending on substitution position.
OI from COL1A2 glycine substitution in the alpha-2 chain. Generally milder than corresponding COL1A1 mutations but still causes significant fracture risk.
Kniest dysplasia from COL2A1 mutations causing Swiss cheese cartilage. Features short trunk dwarfism, enlarged joints, myopia, and hearing loss.
Marshall syndrome from COL11A1 splice site mutations. Overlaps with Stickler type II but features more severe midfacial hypoplasia and hearing loss.

condition: Classical Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome with Vascular Complications
description: Classical EDS variant with atypical vascular complications from COL5A1 mutations. Overlap phenotype with skin and joint features plus arterial fragility.
Infantile cortical hyperostosis from COL1A1 R836C mutation. Self-limiting condition with fever, soft tissue swelling, and subperiosteal new bone formation.
Aortic aneurysm from TGFB2 haploinsufficiency. Variable expressivity with risk of thoracic aortic dissection requiring imaging surveillance.

condition: Generalized Arterial Calcification of Infancy, ABCC6-Related
description: Severe vascular calcification in infancy from ABCC6 mutations overlapping PXE spectrum. Can cause cardiovascular compromise and failure to thrive.
SVAS from elastin gene deletion or mutation causing aortic narrowing. May occur as isolated defect or as part of Williams syndrome spectrum.
Severe cutis laxa from fibulin-5 mutations. Features generalized loose skin, pulmonary emphysema, and vascular tortuosity with supravalvular aortic stenosis.
Cutis laxa type 1B from fibulin-4 deficiency. Features severe aortic aneurysms, arterial tortuosity, and loose redundant skin with emphysema.
Allelic to kyphoscoliotic EDS, Nevo syndrome features increased birth length, kyphosis, joint laxity, and distinctive facial features from PLOD1 mutations.
Isolated familial aortic aneurysm from COL3A1 mutations without full vascular EDS phenotype. Requires aortic surveillance and family screening.
WMS from specific FBN1 mutations causing short stature, brachydactyly, and microspherophakia. Paradoxically opposite to Marfan skeletal features.
FBN1 mutation causing hard, thick skin with limited joint mobility. Progressive fibrosis of subcutaneous tissue without systemic vascular involvement.
Classical-like EDS from complete tenascin-X deficiency. Generalized joint hypermobility, velvety skin, and easy bruising without atrophic scarring.
Milder dermatosparaxis EDS variant with partial ADAMTS2 activity. Reduced but present skin fragility with less severe sagging than classical dermatosparaxis.
OSMED from recessive COL11A2 mutations. Features sensorineural hearing loss, enlarged epiphyses, and disproportionate short stature without ocular involvement.
Congenital myopathy with connective tissue features from FKBP14 mutations. Muscle weakness with skin hyperextensibility and joint hypermobility.
FOP variant with atypical ACVR1 mutation and modified clinical course. May lack classic great toe malformation but develops heterotopic ossification.
Classical CAH from severe CYP21A2 mutations causing cortisol deficiency. Salt-wasting form presents with life-threatening adrenal crisis in neonates.
Non-classical CAH with mild CYP21A2 mutations. Presents with hyperandrogenism, premature pubarche, acne, and infertility in females.
Simple virilizing CAH without salt wasting. Sufficient aldosterone production but cortisol deficiency causes virilization of female genitalia.
CAH from CYP11B1 deficiency causing cortisol deficiency with hypertension. Excess deoxycorticosterone leads to mineralocorticoid hypertension and virilization.
Rare CAH from CYP17A1 mutations causing combined 17-hydroxylase/17,20-lyase deficiency. Hypertension with sexual infantilism in 46,XY and 46,XX individuals.
CAH from HSD3B2 mutations disrupting steroidogenesis. Salt-wasting with incomplete masculinization in males and mild virilization in females.
Constitutively activating TSHR mutations causing thyrotoxicosis without autoimmune antibodies. Presents in childhood with goiter and growth acceleration.
Loss-of-function TSHR mutations causing TSH resistance. Ranges from compensated hypothyroidism to severe congenital hypothyroidism with thyroid hypoplasia.
Thyroid dysgenesis from PAX8 mutations affecting thyroid gland development. Variable phenotype from ectopic thyroid to complete athyreosis.
NKX2-1 haploinsufficiency causing hypothyroidism, respiratory distress, and benign hereditary chorea. Variable expressivity across the triad of features.
FOXE1 mutations causing thyroid agenesis with cleft palate, choanal atresia, and spiky hair. Severe congenital hypothyroidism requiring lifelong replacement.
Congenital hypothyroidism from thyroid peroxidase deficiency. Goitrous hypothyroidism with total iodide organification defect requiring thyroid hormone replacement.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 3
description: MODY3 from HNF1A mutations, the most common MODY form. Progressive beta-cell dysfunction with sensitivity to sulfonylureas as first-line treatment.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 1
description: MODY1 from HNF4A mutations causing progressive insulin secretory defect. Macrosomia at birth followed by diabetes, responsive to sulfonylureas.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 2
description: MODY2 from glucokinase mutations causing stable mild fasting hyperglycemia. Benign course rarely requiring treatment, present from birth.
Neonatal diabetes from heterozygous INS mutations causing misfolded proinsulin and beta-cell ER stress. Presents within first 6 months of life.
Neonatal diabetes from activating KCNJ11 mutations keeping KATP channels open. Many patients transferable from insulin to sulfonylurea therapy.
Neonatal diabetes from activating ABCC8/SUR1 mutations. KATP channel dysfunction prevents insulin secretion but may respond to sulfonylurea therapy.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 4
description: MODY4 from PDX1 mutations affecting pancreatic development and insulin gene transcription. Rare form with variable age of onset and severity.
Albright hereditary osteodystrophy with PTH resistance from maternal GNAS mutations. Features brachydactyly, obesity, round face, and subcutaneous ossifications.
Albright hereditary osteodystrophy from paternal GNAS mutations without hormone resistance. Physical features present but calcium and phosphate levels are normal.
PHP1B from GNAS methylation defects causing renal PTH resistance. Hypocalcemia and hyperphosphatemia without Albright hereditary osteodystrophy features.

condition: Isolated Growth Hormone Deficiency Type II
description: Dominant GHD from GH1 splice site mutations producing dominant-negative GH. Progressive short stature with potential evolution to combined pituitary hormone deficiency.

condition: Isolated Growth Hormone Deficiency Type IA
description: Severe GHD from GH1 gene deletion. Complete absence of GH with severe short stature and risk of anti-GH antibody formation during replacement therapy.

condition: Isolated Growth Hormone Deficiency Type IB, GHRHR-Related
description: GHD from GHRH receptor mutations. Anterior pituitary hypoplasia with proportionate short stature responsive to exogenous GH treatment.
Benign FHH from inactivating CASR mutations. Mild asymptomatic hypercalcemia with inappropriately low urinary calcium excretion not requiring treatment.
Life-threatening neonatal hypercalcemia from homozygous CASR mutations. Requires urgent parathyroidectomy due to severe bone demineralization and failure to thrive.
Congenital hypothyroidism from thyroglobulin gene mutations. Goitrous hypothyroidism with defective thyroglobulin synthesis requiring hormone replacement.
Congenital hypothyroidism from sodium-iodide symporter deficiency. Inability to concentrate iodide in thyroid gland causing goitrous hypothyroidism.
Congenital hypothyroidism from dual oxidase 2 deficiency. Variable severity from transient to permanent hypothyroidism depending on residual enzyme activity.
Iodotyrosine dehalogenase deficiency causing iodide wasting and goitrous hypothyroidism. Impaired iodide recycling leads to progressive thyroid dysfunction.
CAIS from AR loss-of-function mutations in 46,XY individuals. Female external phenotype with absent uterus, inguinal testes, and primary amenorrhea.
PAIS from partial AR dysfunction in 46,XY individuals. Ambiguous genitalia with variable degree of masculinization depending on residual receptor function.
Steroid 5-alpha reductase 2 deficiency in 46,XY individuals. Female-appearing genitalia at birth with virilization at puberty due to testosterone surge.
X-linked Kallmann syndrome from anosmin-1 deficiency. Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism with anosmia due to failed GnRH neuron migration.
Kallmann syndrome from FGFR1 loss-of-function mutations. Variable phenotype with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism, anosmia, and sometimes cleft lip/palate.
Severe salt-wasting CAH from null CYP21A2 alleles. Life-threatening hyponatremic dehydration in neonates without prompt glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid replacement.
Most common genetic cause of combined pituitary hormone deficiency. Progressive loss of GH, TSH, LH/FSH, and PRL secretion with evolving pituitary mass.
Combined deficiency of GH, PRL, and TSH from POU1F1/PIT1 mutations. Severe short stature with central hypothyroidism presenting in early childhood.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 5
description: MODY5 from HNF1B mutations causing diabetes with renal cysts (RCAD syndrome). Progressive renal dysfunction with pancreatic atrophy and genital malformations.

condition: Maturity-Onset Diabetes of the Young, Type 6
description: MODY6 from NEUROD1 mutations affecting beta-cell development and function. Rare form of monogenic diabetes with variable age of onset.
Activating CASR mutations causing inappropriate suppression of PTH secretion. Hypocalcemia with relative hypercalciuria, risk of nephrocalcinosis with vitamin D treatment.
Isolated hypoparathyroidism from GCM2 mutations affecting parathyroid gland development. Hypocalcemia requiring lifelong calcium and vitamin D supplementation.
Mosaic activating GNAS mutations causing fibrous dysplasia, cafe-au-lait spots, and precocious puberty. Somatic mosaicism explains variable presentation.
Congenital hyperinsulinism from inactivating ABCC8 mutations. Persistent hypoglycemia in infancy due to unregulated insulin secretion, may require pancreatectomy.
Congenital hyperinsulinism from inactivating KCNJ11 mutations affecting KATP channel closure. Severe neonatal hypoglycemia often refractory to diazoxide therapy.
Hereditary paraganglioma from SDHB mutations with high malignancy risk. Extra-adrenal paragangliomas and renal cell carcinoma require aggressive surveillance.
Hereditary paraganglioma from SDHD mutations, primarily paternally inherited. Head and neck paragangliomas predominate with lower malignancy risk than SDHB.

condition: Isolated Growth Hormone Deficiency Type III
description: X-linked GHD associated with agammaglobulinemia. Combined immunodeficiency and growth failure requiring both GH replacement and immunoglobulin therapy.

condition: Obesity Due to Prohormone Convertase 1 Deficiency
description: PCSK1 mutations disrupting prohormone processing causing early-onset obesity. Associated with adrenal insufficiency, hypogonadism, and severe malabsorptive diarrhea.

condition: Obesity Due to Congenital Leptin Deficiency
description: Congenital leptin deficiency causing severe early-onset obesity with hyperphagia. Treatable with recombinant leptin therapy leading to dramatic weight reduction.

condition: Obesity Due to Leptin Receptor Deficiency
description: Leptin receptor deficiency causing severe obesity with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism. Unlike leptin deficiency, not responsive to recombinant leptin therapy.
Most common monogenic obesity from MC4R mutations. Hyperphagia with increased linear growth and hyperinsulinemia, variable penetrance with age.

condition: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease, PKD1-Related
description: ADPKD from PKD1 mutations accounting for 85% of cases. Progressive bilateral renal cysts leading to ESRD typically by age 55-60 with hepatic cysts and hypertension.

condition: Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease, PKD2-Related
description: ADPKD from PKD2 mutations with milder course than PKD1. ESRD onset approximately 15 years later than PKD1, around age 70-75.
Infantile nephronophthisis from inversin mutations. Rapid progression to ESRD before age 3 with situs inversus in some patients.
Adolescent nephronophthisis from NPHP3 mutations. Later onset with ESRD in late teenage years, may include retinitis pigmentosa (Senior-Loken syndrome).
Nephronophthisis type 4 from nephroretinin mutations. Juvenile onset with tubulointerstitial fibrosis and frequent association with retinal degeneration.
Senior-Loken syndrome from IQCB1 mutations combining nephronophthisis with retinitis pigmentosa. Progressive blindness accompanies renal failure.
X-linked Alport syndrome from COL4A5 mutations. Progressive nephritis with sensorineural hearing loss and anterior lenticonus. Males reach ESRD by age 20-30.
Autosomal recessive Alport from COL4A3 mutations causing absent type IV collagen alpha-3 chain. Similar severity to X-linked males with ESRD and hearing loss.
Autosomal recessive Alport from COL4A4 mutations. Progressive glomerulonephritis with basement membrane splitting, hearing loss, and ocular abnormalities.
Heterozygous COL4A3 mutation causing benign familial hematuria. Thin glomerular basement membrane with persistent microscopic hematuria but usually benign course.
Antenatal Bartter syndrome from NKCC2 transporter mutations. Severe polyhydramnios, premature birth, salt wasting with hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis.
Antenatal Bartter from ROMK channel mutations. Transient neonatal hyperkalemia followed by hypokalemia, polyhydramnios, and nephrocalcinosis.
Classical Bartter syndrome from ClC-Kb chloride channel mutations. Childhood onset with salt wasting, hypokalemia, and metabolic alkalosis.
Bartter syndrome with sensorineural deafness from barttin mutations. Combined renal salt wasting and hearing loss due to defective chloride channel function.
Infantile nephropathic cystinosis from cystinosin mutations. Renal Fanconi syndrome by age 6 months progressing to ESRD by age 10 without cysteamine treatment.
Intermediate cystinosis with later onset and slower progression than infantile form. Renal failure typically in teenage years with milder Fanconi syndrome.
PH1 from alanine-glyoxylate aminotransferase deficiency. Recurrent calcium oxalate nephrolithiasis and nephrocalcinosis leading to ESRD and systemic oxalosis.
PH2 from glyoxylate/hydroxypyruvate reductase deficiency. Milder than PH1 with recurrent kidney stones but lower risk of systemic oxalosis.
PH3 from 4-hydroxy-2-oxoglutarate aldolase deficiency. Mildest form with early childhood stones that may decrease with age.
Classic Fabry from alpha-galactosidase A deficiency causing globotriaosylceramide accumulation. Progressive renal failure, cardiomyopathy, and stroke with angiokeratoma and acroparesthesias.
Later-onset Fabry with residual GLA activity. Primarily cardiac and renal manifestations in adulthood without classic childhood symptoms.

condition: Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis, Autosomal Dominant
description: Dominant dRTA from band 3 protein mutations. Inability to acidify urine causing metabolic acidosis, nephrocalcinosis, and kidney stone formation.

condition: Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis, Autosomal Recessive with Hearing Loss
description: Recessive dRTA from proton pump mutations with sensorineural hearing loss. Severe metabolic acidosis, growth failure, and rickets in early childhood.

condition: Distal Renal Tubular Acidosis with Early Hearing Loss
description: Recessive dRTA from B1 subunit mutations with early-onset sensorineural deafness. Metabolic acidosis with nephrocalcinosis and progressive hearing loss.
Combined proximal and distal RTA from carbonic anhydrase II deficiency. Mixed RTA with osteopetrosis, cerebral calcification, and intellectual disability.
Dent disease from ClC-5 chloride channel mutations. Low molecular weight proteinuria, hypercalciuria, nephrolithiasis, and progressive renal failure in males.
Lowe syndrome from OCRL mutations. Congenital cataracts, intellectual disability, and renal Fanconi syndrome with progressive renal failure.
CNF from nephrin mutations. Massive proteinuria presenting at birth or in utero. Requires aggressive nutritional support and bilateral nephrectomy with transplantation.
FSGS from podocin mutations causing steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome. Childhood onset with progression to ESRD but low recurrence risk after transplantation.
Dominant FSGS from alpha-actinin-4 mutations. Adult-onset proteinuria with progressive renal insufficiency leading to ESRD over 10-20 years.
FSGS from gain-of-function TRPC6 mutations. Adult-onset proteinuria and progressive renal failure from podocyte calcium channel dysfunction.

condition: Polycystic Kidney Disease, PKD1 Truncating Variant
description: Severe ADPKD from PKD1 truncating mutations with earliest ESRD onset. Higher risk of intracranial aneurysm and more aggressive cyst growth rate.
Non-Finnish CNS from NPHS1 missense mutations with variable severity. May present later than Finnish type with some response to immunosuppressive therapy.
Denys-Drash from WT1 mutations causing diffuse mesangial sclerosis. Triad of nephrotic syndrome, 46,XY disorders of sex development, and Wilms tumor.
Frasier syndrome from WT1 splice mutations. FSGS with 46,XY gonadal dysgenesis and high risk of gonadoblastoma but lower Wilms tumor risk.
Papillorenal syndrome from PAX2 mutations. Optic nerve coloboma with renal hypodysplasia and vesicoureteral reflux, variable progression to ESRD.
RCAD from HNF1B mutations causing renal cysts, MODY5 diabetes, and genital tract anomalies. Highly variable renal phenotype from cysts to renal agenesis.
Milder X-linked Alport from non-truncating COL4A5 mutations. Later ESRD onset with variable hearing loss and thin basement membrane phenotype in carriers.
Severe Gitelman variant with profound magnesium wasting. Tetany, chondrocalcinosis, and cardiac arrhythmias from critically low magnesium despite supplementation.

condition: Cystinuria, Type A, with Early Onset
description: Early-onset cystinuria with severe stone burden from SLC3A1 null mutations. Recurrent bilateral staghorn calculi requiring multiple urologic interventions.
Benign cystinosis with cystine crystal deposits limited to cornea and conjunctiva. No renal involvement, diagnosed incidentally on ophthalmologic examination.
Dent disease variant with prominent hypophosphatemic rickets from severe proximal tubular dysfunction. Combines nephrolithiasis with bone disease.
Dent disease type 2 from OCRL mutations without full Lowe syndrome features. Renal Fanconi syndrome with nephrocalcinosis but milder extrarenal manifestations.
PH1 variant responsive to vitamin B6 supplementation. Specific AGXT missense mutations allow partial enzyme rescue with pyridoxine cofactor therapy.
UAKD from uromodulin/Tamm-Horsfall protein mutations. Progressive tubulointerstitial nephritis with hyperuricemia and gout, ESRD typically by age 40-70.
Renal tubular dysgenesis from renin gene mutations. Severe oligohydramnios sequence with refractory hypotension and anuria, usually lethal in neonatal period.
Renal hypouricemia from URAT1 transporter mutations. Very low serum uric acid with exercise-induced acute kidney injury from uric acid crystal precipitation.
Severe antenatal Bartter type 1 with massive polyhydramnios and extreme prematurity. Life-threatening electrolyte derangements requiring intensive neonatal management.

condition: Bartter Syndrome Type 2 with Transient Hyperkalemia
description: Bartter type 2 variant with prominent neonatal hyperkalemia before typical hypokalemia develops. May mimic pseudohypoaldosteronism in the first weeks of life.
Late-onset SRNS from hypomorphic NPHS2 variants including R229Q. Adolescent or adult presentation with better renal prognosis than childhood-onset disease.
Pierson syndrome from laminin beta-2 mutations. Congenital nephrotic syndrome with microcoria and neurodevelopmental abnormalities, often fatal in infancy.
Early-onset nephrotic syndrome from phospholipase C epsilon-1 mutations. Diffuse mesangial sclerosis or FSGS, some patients respond to immunosuppressive therapy.
FSGS from CD2AP mutations affecting podocyte slit diaphragm. Adult-onset proteinuria progressing to ESRD, susceptibility gene with incomplete penetrance.
FSGS from inverted formin-2 mutations, most common genetic cause of autosomal dominant FSGS. May include Charcot-Marie-Tooth neuropathy in some families.
Joubert syndrome variant with NPHP1 involvement. Molar tooth sign on MRI with cerebellar vermis hypoplasia, plus renal cystic disease progressing to ESRD.

condition: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome, Classical Type, COL5A2 Splice Variant
description: Classical EDS from COL5A2 splice site mutation producing aberrant type V collagen. Moderate skin and joint involvement with variable wound healing.
OI from C-propeptide COL1A1 mutations causing high bone mass OI variant. Paradoxical dense bones that fracture due to abnormal collagen incorporation.
FBN1 mutation causing marfanoid habitus with progeroid features and neonatal lipodystrophy. Overlap condition with accelerated aging and connective tissue laxity.
TGFBR1 variants associated with both connective tissue features and self-healing keratoacanthomas. Demonstrates pleiotropic TGF-beta pathway effects on multiple tissues.
POMC deficiency causing early-onset obesity, adrenal insufficiency, and red hair. Absent ACTH and alpha-MSH from failed proopiomelanocortin processing.
Neonatal isolated ACTH deficiency from TPIT/TBX19 mutations. Life-threatening cortisol deficiency with prolonged neonatal cholestatic jaundice.
X-linked NDI from vasopressin V2 receptor mutations. Severe polyuria and polydipsia from renal resistance to ADH, risk of hypernatremic dehydration in infancy.
Autosomal NDI from aquaporin-2 water channel mutations. Inability to concentrate urine despite elevated ADH levels, requiring high fluid intake and thiazide therapy.
DIDMOAD syndrome from wolframin mutations. Diabetes insipidus, juvenile-onset diabetes mellitus, optic atrophy, and sensorineural deafness with progressive neurodegeneration.
Transient neonatal diabetes from milder KCNJ11 activating mutations. Diabetes resolves in infancy but may relapse in adolescence or adulthood.
Complete glucokinase deficiency from homozygous GCK mutations. Permanent neonatal diabetes requiring insulin from birth, distinct from mild heterozygous MODY2.
Isolated hypoparathyroidism from PTH gene mutations causing defective parathyroid hormone. Hypocalcemia with hyperphosphatemia requiring calcium and calcitriol.
Progressive nephropathy from non-muscle myosin heavy chain IIA mutations. FSGS with macrothrombocytopenia, hearing loss, and leukocyte inclusions (Epstein/Fechtner).
Townes-Brocks syndrome from SALL1 mutations with renal dysplasia. Imperforate anus, dysplastic ears, and thumb anomalies with variable renal hypoplasia to ESRD.
Renal hypodysplasia from SIX2 mutations affecting nephron progenitor self-renewal. Small kidneys with reduced nephron number and progressive renal insufficiency.
Classical Bartter type 3 variant with severe nephrocalcinosis. Hypokalemic alkalosis with prominent calcium deposition in renal medulla causing progressive kidney damage.
Lethal skeletal dysplasia with extremely short limbs, narrow thorax, and curved femora. Caused by activating mutations in FGFR3.
Severe lethal skeletal dysplasia featuring cloverleaf skull deformity and straight femora. Distinct from type I by skull morphology.
Skeletal disorder affecting epiphyseal development leading to early-onset osteoarthritis, short stature, and joint pain. Caused by mutations in cartilage oligomeric matrix protein.
Epiphyseal dysplasia caused by defective type IX collagen. Presents with joint stiffness, waddling gait, and premature osteoarthritis.
Variant of MED caused by COL9A3 mutations affecting type IX collagen alpha-3 chain. Features hip and knee pain with mild short stature.
Skeletal dysplasia caused by matrilin-3 mutations affecting cartilage extracellular matrix. Presents with early-onset knee pain and hand brachydactyly.
Lethal skeletal dysplasia caused by sulfate transporter deficiency. Features severely shortened limbs, dislocated joints, and gap between first and second toes.
Lethal neonatal skeletal disorder with extreme limb shortening, barrel-shaped thorax, and severely undermineralized skeleton.
Chondroectodermal dysplasia caused by EVC2 mutations with features identical to EVC-related disease including acromesomelic limb shortening.
X-linked skeletal disorder with stippled epiphyses, nasal hypoplasia, and distal phalangeal hypoplasia. Caused by arylsulfatase E deficiency.
Conradi-Hunermann-Happle syndrome with asymmetric limb shortening, ichthyosis, and cataracts. Lethal in males, variable in females.
Rare skeletal dysplasia with craniosynostosis, prominent supraorbital ridges, depressed nasal bridge, and rhizomelic dwarfism. FGFR1 activating mutations.
Heart-hand syndrome with upper limb defects ranging from triphalangeal thumb to phocomelia and congenital heart defects, especially ASD and VSD.
Malignant infantile osteopetrosis with dense brittle bones, bone marrow failure, cranial nerve compression, and hepatosplenomegaly. Osteoclast dysfunction.
Severe osteopetrosis with generalized osteosclerosis, visual impairment from optic nerve compression, and recurrent fractures despite dense bones.
Albers-Schonberg disease with sclerotic bones, sandwich vertebrae, cranial nerve palsies, and increased fracture susceptibility. Most common osteopetrosis.
Severe infantile osteopetrosis with neurodegeneration, retinal atrophy, and bone marrow failure. OSTM1 stabilizes the CLCN7 chloride channel.
Somatic mosaic disorder replacing normal bone with fibrous tissue. Can affect single or multiple bones, causing pain, deformity, and pathological fractures.
Disorder of accelerated bone remodeling with enlarged deformed bones, pain, and increased fracture risk. SQSTM1 mutations cause familial early-onset form.
Multiple osteochondromas growing near growth plates causing limb deformity, short stature, and risk of malignant transformation to chondrosarcoma.
Multiple cartilage-capped bone tumors near growth plates with limb-length discrepancy and angular deformity. Generally milder than EXT1-related disease.
Mild metaphyseal dysplasia with short stature, bowed legs, and waddling gait. Type X collagen mutations affect hypertrophic chondrocyte zone.
Severe short-limbed dwarfism with hypercalcemia and hypophosphatemia from constitutive PTH receptor activation. Grossly irregular metaphyses.
Severe skeletal dysplasia with kyphoscoliosis converting short-limbed to short-trunk dwarfism over time. TRPV4 calcium channel gain-of-function.
Skeletal dysplasia with short trunk, scoliosis, and flattened vertebral bodies. Milder allelic variant of TRPV4-related conditions.
Short trunk dwarfism with pear-shaped vertebral bodies and metaphyseal irregularities. TRPV4 mutations disrupt calcium signaling in chondrocytes.
Autosomal recessive form of MED with bilateral clubfoot, double-layered patella, and progressive joint degeneration. Milder SLC26A2 allelic spectrum.
Rare type II collagenopathy with platyspondyly, brachydactyly, and short stature. Peripheral skeletal involvement distinguishes it from other COL2A1 disorders.
Mesomelic dysplasia with Madelung deformity of the wrist and mild short stature. SHOX haploinsufficiency affects growth plate development.
Severe mesomelic short stature from complete SHOX deficiency with aplasia of ulna and fibula. Homozygous form of Leri-Weill dyschondrosteosis.
Multiple joint dislocations, characteristic flattened facies, and cervical vertebral abnormalities. Filamin B mutations disrupt cartilage and skeletal development.
Lethal skeletal dysplasia with severe limb shortening, distal tapering of humeri, and joint dislocations. Filamin B gain-of-function mutations.
Lethal skeletal dysplasia with boomerang-shaped long bones, absent ossification of vertebral bodies, and encephalocele. Severe FLNB spectrum.
Progressive joint stiffness and enlargement mimicking juvenile rheumatoid arthritis, with platyspondyly and progressive bone loss. Onset ages 3-8.
Short stature with bowing of lower limbs, enlarged knee joints, and platyspondyly. Defective sulfation of proteoglycans impairs cartilage matrix.
Severe bent-bone dysplasia with camptodactyly, respiratory distress, and autonomic dysfunction. High neonatal mortality due to temperature instability.
Fetal face syndrome with mesomelic limb shortening, genital hypoplasia, and characteristic facial features resembling a fetal face.
Severe form of Robinow syndrome with acral and vertebral anomalies, more pronounced mesomelic shortening, and cardiac defects.
Shortening of middle phalanges with ulnar deviation of index finger and hypersegmentation of proximal phalanges. GDF5 loss-of-function.
Fusion of proximal interphalangeal joints with conductive hearing loss from ankylosis of the stapes. GDF5 gain-of-function mutations.
Progressive joint fusion including vertebral, carpal, and tarsal synostoses with conductive hearing loss. Noggin mutations cause BMP signaling excess.
Craniosynostosis with tarsal and metatarsal coalitions and broad great toes. Milder FGFR2 spectrum disorder with primarily foot abnormalities.
Severe skeletal dysplasia with bowed tibiae, sex reversal in 46,XY individuals, and respiratory insufficiency. Often lethal in neonatal period.
Severe achondroplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans. FGFR3 Lys650Met mutation causes extreme skeletal undergrowth.
Shortening of middle phalanges of digits 2-5, with proximal phalanx of thumb also affected. Indian hedgehog signaling pathway disorder.
Myotonic myopathy with skeletal dysplasia, blepharophimosis, and joint contractures. Perlecan deficiency disrupts cartilage and neuromuscular junction.
I-cell disease with severe skeletal dysplasia, coarse facial features, and cardiomegaly. Defective lysosomal enzyme targeting causes inclusion bodies.
Pseudo-Hurler polydystrophy with progressive joint stiffness, claw hand deformity, and mild coarsening of facies. Milder than ML II.
MPS IVA with severe skeletal dysplasia, short trunk dwarfism, odontoid hypoplasia causing cervical instability, and corneal clouding.
MPS IVB with milder skeletal dysplasia than type A, corneal clouding, and keratan sulfate accumulation. Beta-galactosidase deficiency.
Progressive retinal degeneration with night blindness, tunnel vision, and eventual blindness. Rhodopsin mutations are the most common cause of adRP.
Most common RP mutation in North America. Causes rod photoreceptor misfolding leading to progressive degeneration and visual field constriction.
Progressive retinal degeneration from photoreceptor connecting cilium dysfunction. Variable onset with night blindness typically preceding visual field loss.
Severe early-onset RP in males with rapid progression to legal blindness by age 30-40. RPGR mutations account for 70-80% of X-linked RP.
X-linked RP with early-onset macular atrophy distinguishing it from RPGR disease. RP2 protein functions in ciliary trafficking.
RP with incomplete penetrance due to expression level variation of the wild-type allele. Pre-mRNA splicing factor essential for photoreceptor survival.
RP or pattern dystrophy caused by peripherin-2 mutations affecting photoreceptor outer segment disc morphogenesis. Highly variable phenotype.
Most common Usher syndrome with moderate-to-severe congenital hearing loss, normal vestibular function, and postpubertal RP onset.
Profound congenital deafness with vestibular areflexia and early RP. Cadherin-23 is essential for hair cell stereocilia tip links.
Progressive hearing loss, variable vestibular dysfunction, and RP. Higher prevalence in Finnish and Ashkenazi Jewish populations. Clarin-1 protein disorder.
Progressive cone then rod photoreceptor degeneration with early loss of central and color vision. Allelic to Stargardt disease.
Severe childhood blindness from retinal pigment epithelium dysfunction. Treatable with voretigene neparvovec gene therapy (Luxturna).
Most common LCA type with severe vision loss from birth due to ciliopathy. CEP290 intronic mutation creates aberrant splice site. Antisense therapy in trials.
Severe early-onset retinal dystrophy with nystagmus and amaurotic pupils. Retinal guanylate cyclase deficiency disrupts phototransduction.
Early-onset retinal dystrophy with preserved para-arteriolar retinal pigment epithelium, nummular pigmentation, and macular coloboma.
Nonprogressive night blindness from ON-bipolar cell signaling defect. Complete form with absent rod and reduced cone ERG b-wave.
Complete CSNB from metabotropic glutamate receptor 6 mutations. Defective ON-bipolar cell signaling with electronegative ERG.
Complete color blindness with severe photophobia, nystagmus, and reduced visual acuity. CNGB3 cone cyclic nucleotide-gated channel dysfunction.
Total color blindness from cone photoreceptor dysfunction with nystagmus and extreme photosensitivity. Gene therapy trials showing promise.
Ciliopathy with retinal dystrophy, obesity, polydactyly, renal anomalies, and cognitive impairment. Most common BBS gene.
Multisystem ciliopathy with progressive rod-cone dystrophy, truncal obesity, and renal dysfunction. BBS2 is a core BBSome component.
BBS with chaperonin-like protein dysfunction affecting BBSome assembly. Second most commonly mutated BBS gene.
Ciliopathy with retinal degeneration, polydactyly, and renal structural anomalies. BBS4 protein involved in intracellular trafficking.
Most common cause of inherited sensorineural hearing loss. Connexin-26 gap junction dysfunction disrupts potassium recycling in cochlea.
Most prevalent GJB2 mutation in European populations causing moderate to profound hearing loss. Frameshift deletes functional connexin-26.
Hearing loss from connexin-30 dysfunction, often compound heterozygous with GJB2 mutations. Large deletions disrupting cochlear gap junctions.
Prelingual sensorineural hearing loss with enlarged vestibular aqueduct but without thyroid goiter. Allelic to Pendred syndrome.
Pigmentary anomalies and hearing loss without dystopia canthorum. MITF transcription factor controls melanocyte development and survival.
Waardenburg-Shah syndrome combining WS features with Hirschsprung disease. SOX10 mutations affect neural crest-derived melanocytes and enteric ganglia.
Prelingual profound sensorineural hearing loss from transmembrane channel-like 1 dysfunction. TMC1 forms the mechanotransduction channel of hair cells.
Prelingual profound deafness with auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder. Otoferlin essential for synaptic vesicle exocytosis at inner hair cell ribbon synapse.
Profound congenital hearing loss from unconventional myosin XV dysfunction. Essential for stereocilia elongation and staircase pattern formation.
Postlingual progressive hearing loss or prelingual profound deafness. Transmembrane serine protease essential for cochlear hair cell survival.
Moderate hearing loss with adolescent-onset RP and normal vestibular function. Whirlin protein essential for stereocilia and photoreceptor structure.
Profound sensorineural hearing loss from whirlin dysfunction without retinal involvement. Allelic to Usher 2D but milder phenotype.
Severe to profound prelingual hearing loss from disrupted stereocilia rootlet formation. TRIOBP organizes F-actin in hair cell stereocilia.
Midfrequency hearing loss affecting primarily 1-2 kHz range. Alpha-tectorin mutations disrupt tectorial membrane structure in cochlea.
Low-frequency sensorineural hearing loss affecting primarily 250-2000 Hz. Wolframin protein dysfunction in endoplasmic reticulum calcium signaling.
Progressive high-frequency hearing loss from potassium channel dysfunction in outer hair cells. Gradual deterioration over decades.
Mixed hearing loss with stapes fixation and perilymphatic gusher during surgery. POU3F4 transcription factor essential for otic capsule development.
Postlingual progressive hearing loss from PLAT-domain protein dysfunction in stereocilia. Mechanosensory hair cell degeneration over time.
Sensorineural hearing loss from espin deficiency causing shortened stereocilia. Espin cross-links and bundles actin filaments in hair cell stereocilia.
Auditory neuropathy from pejvakin dysfunction. Pejvakin protects auditory pathway neurons and hair cells from oxidative stress damage.
X-linked retinal degeneration with variable severity in carrier females. Night blindness progresses to peripheral and central vision loss.
Egg yolk-like macular lesion with progressive central vision loss. Bestrophin-1 chloride channel dysfunction in retinal pigment epithelium.
Bilateral foveal schisis with vitreous veils and risk of retinal detachment. Retinoschisin dysfunction disrupts retinal cell adhesion.
Progressive chorioretinal degeneration with night blindness and tunnel vision in males. REP1 deficiency impairs Rab prenylation in RPE.
Congenital bilateral blindness from retinal pseudotumors, progressive hearing loss, and intellectual disability. Norrin-FZD4 signaling disrupted.
Rod photoreceptor degeneration from cyclic nucleotide-gated channel alpha-1 subunit dysfunction. Progressive night blindness with visual field constriction.
RP from rod phosphodiesterase beta subunit deficiency causing constitutive cGMP signaling and rod cell death.
Rod phosphodiesterase alpha subunit deficiency leading to cGMP accumulation and photoreceptor apoptosis. Early-onset severe RP.
Early-onset severe RP from tubby-like protein 1 dysfunction. TULP1 essential for rhodopsin vesicular transport in photoreceptors.
One of the most common arRP genes. Eyes shut homolog protein essential for photoreceptor outer segment structure and stability.
Severe early-onset retinal dystrophy from retinol dehydrogenase 12 deficiency. Impaired visual cycle retinoid metabolism in photoreceptors.
Severe LCA with early-onset nystagmus and keratoconus. AIPL1 co-chaperone essential for rod phosphodiesterase stability.
Early-onset severe retinal dystrophy from RPGR-interacting protein dysfunction. RPGRIP1 anchors RPGR at the photoreceptor connecting cilium.
Cone-rod homeobox transcription factor mutations causing severe LCA or late-onset cone-rod dystrophy. CRX essential for photoreceptor gene expression.
LCA from IQ calmodulin-binding motif-containing protein dysfunction at the photoreceptor cilium. Some patients develop nephronophthisis (Senior-Loken syndrome).
Severe early-onset retinal degeneration from spermatogenesis-associated protein 7 loss. SPATA7 localizes to photoreceptor connecting cilia.
LCA with macular coloboma-like atrophy. NMNAT1 nicotinamide mononucleotide adenylyltransferase essential for NAD biosynthesis in photoreceptors.
Immotile cilia syndrome with chronic sinopulmonary infections, bronchiectasis, and situs inversus in 50%. Outer dynein arm deficiency.
Most common PCD type with absent outer dynein arms. Chronic productive cough, recurrent pneumonia, and male infertility from immotile sperm.
PCD with normal ultrastructure on electron microscopy. Outer dynein arm dysfunction detectable only by high-speed video microscopy.
PCD with inner dynein arm defects and ciliary disorganization. CCDC39 is a coiled-coil domain protein essential for axonemal organization.
PCD with inner dynein arm and central apparatus defects. CCDC40 works with CCDC39 to establish the 96-nm axonemal repeat.
PCD with central pair defects. Radial spoke head protein 4A dysfunction disrupts mechanochemical signal transduction in cilia.
PCD with central microtubular pair transposition defect. Associated with reduced situs inversus frequency compared to outer dynein arm PCD.
PCD from dynein arm assembly factor deficiency. DNAAF1 is a cytoplasmic chaperone for outer dynein arm preassembly.
PCD with absent outer and inner dynein arms. DNAAF2 (KTU) is required for cytoplasmic preassembly of dynein arm complexes.
PCD with combined outer and inner dynein arm defects. LRRC6 is a leucine-rich repeat protein for dynein arm assembly.
PCD from zinc finger MYND domain protein deficiency causing absent outer and inner dynein arms. Required for cytoplasmic dynein assembly.
PCD with outer dynein arm attachment defect. CCDC103 is a coiled-coil domain protein required for dynein arm docking.
Lethal neonatal respiratory failure from surfactant protein B deficiency. SP-B essential for lamellar body formation and surface tension reduction.
Interstitial lung disease from misfolded surfactant protein C causing ER stress and type II alveolar cell toxicity. Variable onset from infancy to adulthood.
Neonatal respiratory distress or childhood interstitial lung disease from lamellar body biogenesis defect. ABCA3 transports lipids into lamellar bodies.
Brain-lung-thyroid syndrome with respiratory distress, choreoathetosis, and congenital hypothyroidism. NKX2-1 haploinsufficiency.
Accumulation of lipoproteinaceous material in alveoli from defective GM-CSF signaling. Impaired alveolar macrophage surfactant catabolism.
Alveolar proteinosis from defective GM-CSF receptor beta chain. Progressive dyspnea with crazy-paving pattern on chest CT.
Progressive pulmonary vascular remodeling with right heart failure. BMPR2 mutations account for 70% of familial PAH with 20% penetrance.
Severe early-onset PAH from BMPR2 truncating mutation. TGF-beta superfamily signaling disruption leads to pulmonary vascular smooth muscle proliferation.
PAH associated with hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia. ALK1/ACVRL1 mutations cause both vascular malformations and pulmonary vascular disease.
PAH in context of HHT type 1 with pulmonary arteriovenous malformations. Endoglin coreceptor for TGF-beta superfamily.
Mild CFTR variant with residual chloride channel function. Phenotype depends on thymidine tract polymorphism in intron 8 (5T/7T/9T).
CFTR gating mutation responsive to ivacaftor therapy. Defective channel opening with normal CFTR trafficking to cell surface.
Severe CFTR missense mutation with defective protein processing and gating. Third most common CF mutation worldwide.
Nonsense mutation common in Ashkenazi Jewish population producing truncated non-functional CFTR. Class I mutation with no protein produced.
Premature stop codon producing truncated CFTR protein. Severe phenotype with pancreatic insufficiency and progressive lung disease.
Splicing mutation producing reduced levels of normal CFTR. Variable phenotype from mild to severe depending on residual transcript levels.
Deep intronic CFTR mutation creating cryptic splice site. Mild pulmonary phenotype with normal pancreatic function and later onset.
MUC5B promoter polymorphism is the strongest genetic risk factor for IPF. Paradoxically associated with better survival once fibrosis develops.
Progressive cystic lung destruction from proliferation of smooth muscle-like LAM cells. TSC1/hamartin loss activates mTOR signaling. Sirolimus effective.
Cystic lung disease from tuberin/TSC2 loss causing mTOR hyperactivation. Almost exclusively affects women. Can be sporadic or TSC-associated.
